Subject: Re: exclude hosts from reverse DNS checks
Body: On Sat, Feb 09, 2008 at 12:09:17PM +0100, mouss wrote: > do you mean something like > check_client_access pcre:/etc/postfix/clt > > == clt: > /^unknown$/ do_foo > /./ do_bar > > ? This is not related to the original question, but is it possible to match "anything else" (like you do with /./ in this pcre map) in bdb hash maps? Geert

Subject: NIKT 2024 notification for paper 3
Body: Dear Bianca, We are pleased to inform you that your paper has been ACCEPTED to be presented at the Norwegian Information Security Conference (NISK) on Nov. 25-27, 2024, in Bergen, Norway. The reviews by the Technical Program Committee of your paper follow this message. We urge you to complete the following procedure: 1. closely follow all the reviews and upload your revised paper by Oct. 25th, 2024 (updating your paper in the easychair system) 2. register by Nov. 1st, 2024 using the following link: https://www.nikt2024.no/attending/registration We are looking forward to seeing you in Bergen in November. Thank you again for your contribution to this conference. Sincerely, Martin G. Jaatun Chunlei Li Program Chairs for NISK-2024 SUBMISSION: 3 TITLE: Impact of Emotions on User Behavior Toward Phishing Emails
text:
NIKT 2024 notification for paper 3

Dear Bianca, We are pleased to inform you that your paper has been ACCEPTED to be presented at the Norwegian Information Security Conference (NISK) on Nov. 25-27, 2024, in Bergen, Norway. The reviews by the Technical Program Committee of your paper follow this message. We urge you to complete the following procedure: 1. closely follow all the reviews and upload your revised paper by Oct. 25th, 2024 (updating your paper in the easychair system) 2. register by Nov. 1st, 2024 using the following link: https://www.nikt2024.no/attending/registration We are looking forward to seeing you in Bergen in November. Thank you again for your contribution to this conference. Sincerely, Martin G. Jaatun Chunlei Li Program Chairs for NISK-2024 SUBMISSION: 3 TITLE: Impact of Emotions on User Behavior Toward Phishing Emails

Subject: Re: [perl #46019] Documentation for 'srand' has a problem in code snippet
Body: On 03/10/2007, via RT ben @ linuxgazette. net wrote: > > "perldoc -f srand" (and consequently, perlfunc.pod) contains the following: > > `` > Note that you need something much more random than the default > seed for cryptographic purposes. Checksumming the compressed > output of one or more rapidly changing operating system status > programs is the usual method. For example: > > srand (time ^ $$ ^ unpack "%L*", `ps axww | gzip`); > '' > > The problem is that 'ps axww | gzip' returns the same output every time, at > least on my system: > > `` > ben@Tyr:~$ ps axww|gzip > gzip: compressed data not written to a terminal. Use -f to force compression. > For help, type: gzip -h > '' > > The solution suggested in the error message (i.e., 'ps axww | gzip -f') > works fine. > Thanks, I've updated the docs accordingly.
text:
Re: [perl #46019] Documentation for 'srand' has a problem in code snippet

On 03/10/2007, via RT ben @ linuxgazette. net wrote: > > "perldoc -f srand" (and consequently, perlfunc.pod) contains the following: > > `` > Note that you need something much more random than the default > seed for cryptographic purposes. Checksumming the compressed > output of one or more rapidly changing operating system status > programs is the usual method. For example: > > srand (time ^ $$ ^ unpack "%L*", `ps axww | gzip`); > '' > > The problem is that 'ps axww | gzip' returns the same output every time, at > least on my system: > > `` > ben@Tyr:~$ ps axww|gzip > gzip: compressed data not written to a terminal. Use -f to force compression. > For help, type: gzip -h > '' > > The solution suggested in the error message (i.e., 'ps axww | gzip -f') > works fine. > Thanks, I've updated the docs accordingly.
